‚Äã‚ÄãHealthy Families Solano County‚Ñ¢‚Äã (HFS), we believe that early, nurturing relationships are the foundation for life-long, healthy development. HFS promotes positive parenting and child health and development, thereby preventing child abuse, neglect and other poor childhood outcomes!
3 image collage of diverse family units
What is Healthy Families America¬Æ?
Developed by Prevent Child Abuse America¬Æ, HFA is a free and voluntary home visiting program that embodies the cornerstones of healthy relationships. Once parents experience safety, predictability, comfort, and joy in a genuine way and in the context of a relationship with another, they can provide the same experience for their children.
Locally known as Healthy Families Solano‚Ñ¢ (HFS). The HFS home visiting program matches parents with knowledgeable and caring Family Support Specialists who provide information and support during pregnancy and early childhood. Services include helping families access community resources and services, educating families on parenting and child development, connecting families with medical providers, and assessing children for developmental delays. HFS offers at least one in-person, home visit per week for the first six months after enrollment. After those initial months, visit frequency is based on families' needs.
Program Goals:
Reduce child maltreatment
Improve parent-child interactions and children‚Äôs social-emotional well-being
Increase school readiness
Promote child physical health and development
Promote positive parenting
Promote family self-sufficiency
Increase access to primary care medical services and community services
Decrease child injuries and emergency department use.

What is the Cost for Services?
There is no cost to you, the program is provided free of charge.
